title: Applicability Conditions
summary: Defines which REDD and WRC activity types are eligible under this methodology, including applicability conditions for avoiding planned/unplanned deforestation, peatland rewetting, tidal wetland restoration, and conservation. Includes prohibitions on water table lowering, organic soil burning, and nitrogen fertilizer use.
workflow_role: gatekeeper — must pass all applicability conditions before proceeding to boundary definition

title: Project Boundary
summary: Defines the spatial, temporal, carbon pool, and GHG source boundaries of the project. Includes geographic boundary requirements, stratification, carbon pool selection per activity type, GHG source inclusion/exclusion, historical reference period, and crediting period duration.
workflow_role: foundation — project boundaries must be established before baseline and quantification

title: Baseline Scenario
summary: Determines the most plausible baseline scenario using VT0001 barrier/investment analysis. Defines minimum alternative scenarios, module selection for baseline estimation (BL-PL, BL-UP, BL-PEAT, BL-TW), reassessment frequency, and JNR data use provisions.
workflow_role: baseline — establishes the counterfactual scenario against which emission reductions are measured

title: Additionality
summary: Requires demonstration of additionality using VT0001 for all non-tidal-wetland activities, or ADD-AM for tidal wetland conservation and restoration activities.
workflow_role: eligibility — additionality must be demonstrated before quantification

title: Quantification
summary: Provides equations and procedures for calculating baseline emissions, project emissions, leakage, buffer pool contributions, uncertainty adjustments, and Verified Carbon Units. References carbon pool modules (VMD0001-0005), baseline modules, monitoring modules, and leakage modules.
workflow_role: calculation — core quantification of emission reductions using modular equations

title: Monitoring
summary: Defines monitoring plan requirements including four mandatory tasks, content elements per task, geographic position recording, SOP/QA/QC requirements, forest cover monitoring frequency, WRC compliance monitoring, expert judgment documentation, and uncertainty reduction requirements.
workflow_role: ongoing — monitoring requirements apply throughout the crediting period and at each verification
